summary: Three material issues. The branch adds ranking/harness paths that can either fail to affect the real candidate set or leave the normal daemon index in an ablated state.

findings.title: Ablation runs rewrite the real daemon indexes
findings.body: `run_search_matrix_command` uses `DaemonPaths::new()` for the baseline runner, so the harness points at the normal `~/.julie` home. For non-baseline ablations, `run_baseline_async` then force-runs `ManageWorkspaceTool { operation: "index", force: true }` under the ablation env vars. That rewrites the registered daemon workspace's Tantivy projection with an ablated tokenizer and does not restore the baseline projection afterward. Impact: a measurement command can leave the developer's normal Julie search serving or reopening against ablated on-disk indexes, with no isolation or coordination with a live daemon.
findings.recommendation: Run ablations against an isolated temporary Julie home/index root, or restore every affected workspace with a baseline force-reindex before returning. Do not default ablation writes to `DaemonPaths::new()`.

findings.title: Files-target title boost cannot rescue missing symbol-name candidates
findings.body: The files boost is applied only after `search_files` has already queried file path/basename/path_text fields and truncated results. A file whose path does not contain the query but whose symbols do match the query is never returned from `search_files`, so `apply_symbol_title_boost_to_file_results` never sees it. The new test hand-builds the matching file into the candidate list, so it misses the real failure path. Impact: the claimed files-target fix for symbol/title-shaped queries can be a no-op in the real pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Merge symbol-title candidates before final file-target truncation, or run a DB/title lookup that can add matching files to the candidate set. Add a real pipeline test where the matching file path lacks the query terms.

findings.title: Compound title queries boost generic one-word symbols instead of exact concepts
findings.body: Both files/content title boosts compare each lowercased stored symbol name against any individual query term. For a query like `display template`, a symbol named `displayTemplate` becomes `displaytemplate` and receives no boost, while a generic symbol named `display` does receive `EXACT_TITLE_BOOST`. Impact: multi-token symbol-intent queries can promote the wrong file and miss the exact compound concept the user asked for.
findings.recommendation: Normalize compound queries and symbol names to a comparable form, e.g. compare `displaytemplate` to `displayTemplate` lower/compact, or require all query terms to match a tokenized symbol title before applying the boost. Cover the same behavior in content and files tests.

next_steps: Isolate or restore ablation indexes before using the harness on registered daemon workspaces.
Move files-target title matching before candidate truncation and make it capable of adding symbol-title files.
Replace any-term title boost tests with compound-query real pipeline regressions.
